violations.text: Rice, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025
Rice, dated 11/8/2025, was observed being held past (7) days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of employee, rice was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling methods reviewed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025

The presence of rodents and roach are not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings and dead roaches. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025
The prep unit located in the kitchen was not in good repair. (Ambient 44) PIC adjusted unit during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the hood/ventilation unit have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, cheese sauce was placed warm in the prep unit to cool. Inspector informed PIC that prep unit insufficient for cooling, PIC place sauce in refrigerator to complete cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2024

An employee was observed washing their hands at the prep sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (hotel pans and lids) were observed with labels and stickers. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch and free of labels. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

Multiple foods in the prep unit were observed held between 47- and 51-degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were disposed. Foods within temperature control moved to other refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024
The prep unit located in the kitchen area was not in good repair, observed with an ambient temperature of 48 degrees Fahrenheit. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2024
Inspector observed dishes being washed in second bay of food prep sink due to grease trap not working properly. Per PIC grease trap is to be repaired tomorrow. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2024
Grease trap observed in poor repair. Per PIC interview, part to arrive tomorrow. PIC to contact inspector when repair has been made. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2024
There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings in the basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC informed to obtain pest control services. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023
Water observed in basement. Per interview with PIC, leak is coming from window and is to be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023
